title: Quality Observation
description: An observation of a person drawing on (or overextending) a Human Atlas core quality. Models what was observed and keeps interpretation strictly separate. Non-clinical: never a diagnosis, never a fixed personality label.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the human-mind schema family this document conforms to.

construct.description: Marks this as a Human Atlas quality observation, distinct from the twelve cognitive-module observations.

quality.description: The English quality id this observation is about, e.g. 'decisive'. Must match an entry in models/human-atlas/qualities/core-qualities.en.json.

observation.description: What was actually observed, in plain language. Description only — no inference, no label.

indicators.description: Observable markers, drawn from the quality's indicators. Each is a thing you can point at.

context.description: Situational frame. Helps later interpretation without contaminating the observation.

confidence.description: Confidence in the OBSERVATION being accurate — not in any interpretation.

interpretation.description: OPTIONAL and strictly separate. A labelled hypothesis about what the observation might mean, typically a quadrant projection. Never a diagnosis. Its confidence must not exceed the observation's.

quadrantPosition.description: Where on the quality's quadrant this behaviour reads, if a quadrant lens is used.

confidence.description: Confidence in the INTERPRETATION. Must be <= observation confidence.

provenance.description: Where this observation came from and who recorded it.
